mnemonics.title: T F O - Technical Financial Organizational
mnemonics.content: Remember the three main issue categories: Technical (infrastructure, legacy), Financial (costs, budgets), Organizational (people, processes). Think: 'TFO - The Fabulous Organization'

acronyms.usage: Measures the profitability of capacity planning investments
Metrics used to evaluate capacity planning success
Defines expected service performance that capacity must support
Complete cost perspective including acquisition, operation, and maintenance
Unit of capacity that needs to be planned in virtualized environments

visualTips.visualization: Picture a scale: too much weight on one side (wasted money) vs too little (system crashes). Aim for balance in the middle.
Imagine a weather forecast vs actual weather - both show patterns but prediction is never 100% accurate.
Think of a old car - it works but can't be easily upgraded with modern features.
Picture being stuck in a room with only one door that has a heavy lock - difficult to leave.

analogies.analogy: Like a restaurant managing kitchen capacity - too many chefs wastes money, too few causes bad service.
Trying to predict how many customers will visit a store - affected by weather, holidays, promotions, and trends.
Like an apartment building with shared utilities - one tenant's heavy usage affects others.
Using both owned and rented equipment - deciding what to buy vs rent based on needs.
